collections_config.title: Collections Configuration
collections_config.description: Definitions for your collections, which are the sets of content files for your site grouped by folder. Entries are keyed by a chosen collection key, and contain configuration specific to that collection.
collections_config.markdownDescription: Definitions for your collections, which are the sets of content files for your site grouped by folder. Entries are keyed by a chosen collection key, and contain configuration specific to that collection.

url.description: This key defines the output URL for files in a given Collection. CloudCannon uses the output URL in the Visual Editor, and when linking to your Testing Domain and Custom Domain.
url.markdownDescription: This key defines the output URL for files in a given Collection. CloudCannon uses the output URL in the Visual Editor, and when linking to your Testing Domain and Custom Domain.

disable_url.description: This key toggles whether CloudCannon will generate an output URL for a given Collection.
disable_url.markdownDescription: This key toggles whether CloudCannon will generate an output URL for a given Collection.

include_developer_files.description: This key toggles whether CloudCannon removes developer files from your _Collection browser_.
include_developer_files.markdownDescription: This key toggles whether CloudCannon removes developer files from your _Collection browser_.

name.description: This key defines the display name for a Collection. The name appears in the _Site Navigation_ and at the top of the _Collection browser_.
name.markdownDescription: This key defines the display name for a Collection. The name appears in the _Site Navigation_ and at the top of the _Collection browser_.

description.description: This key defines the description text that appears on the _Collection browser_ page. Collection descriptions are useful for adding extra context for your team members.
description.markdownDescription: This key defines the description text that appears on the _Collection browser_ page. Collection descriptions are useful for adding extra context for your team members.

icon.description: This key defines the icon for a Collection. Collection icons appear in the _Site Navigation_ and are the default icon for Collection file Cards if you have not defined `preview.icon`.
icon.markdownDescription: This key defines the icon for a Collection. Collection icons appear in the _Site Navigation_ and are the default icon for Collection file Cards if you have not defined `preview.icon`.

documentation.description: This key defines the documentation link at the top of a _Collection browser_. Collection documentation is useful for assisting your team members.
documentation.markdownDescription: This key defines the documentation link at the top of a _Collection browser_. Collection documentation is useful for assisting your team members.

sort_options.description: This key defines the options for the Sort dropdown in a _Collection browser_. The first option listed is used as the default sort.

items.title: SortOption
items.description: A sort option for a Collection.

label.description: The text to display in the sort option list. Defaults to a generated label from key and order.
label.markdownDescription: The text to display in the sort option list. Defaults to a generated label from key and order.

key.description: Defines what field contains the value to sort on inside each collection item's data.
key.markdownDescription: Defines what field contains the value to sort on inside each collection item's data.

order.description: Controls which sort values come first.
order.markdownDescription: Controls which sort values come first.

items.markdownDescription: A sort option for a Collection.

sort_options.markdownDescription: This key defines the options for the Sort dropdown in a _Collection browser_. The first option listed is used as the default sort.

view_options.description: This key defines the options for the View dropdown in a _Collection browser_. The first option listed is used as the default view.
view_options.markdownDescription: This key defines the options for the View dropdown in a _Collection browser_. The first option listed is used as the default view.

singular_name.description: This key defines the singular noun for your Collection name. CloudCannon uses the singular noun in the _+ Add_ button in the top right of the _Collection browser_.
singular_name.markdownDescription: This key defines the singular noun for your Collection name. CloudCannon uses the singular noun in the _+ Add_ button in the top right of the _Collection browser_.

add_options.description: This key defines the options available in the _+ Add_ button dropdown at the top right of your _Collection browser_.

anyOf.title: Add Option
anyOf.description: An option for the add menu.

name.description: The text displayed for the menu item. Defaults to using name from the matching schema if set.
name.markdownDescription: The text displayed for the menu item. Defaults to using name from the matching schema if set.

icon.description: The icon next to the text in the menu item. Defaults to using icon from the matching schema if set, then falls back to add.
icon.markdownDescription: The icon next to the text in the menu item. Defaults to using icon from the matching schema if set, then falls back to add.

editor.description: The editor to open the new file in. Defaults to an appropriate editor for new file's type if possible. If no default editor can be calculated, or the editor does not support the new file type, a warning is shown in place of the editor.
editor.markdownDescription: The editor to open the new file in. Defaults to an appropriate editor for new file's type if possible. If no default editor can be calculated, or the editor does not support the new file type, a warning is shown in place of the editor.

base_path.description: Enforces a path for new files to be created in, regardless of path the user is currently navigated to within the collection file list. Relative to the path of the collection defined in collection. Defaults to the path within the collection the user is currently navigated to.
base_path.markdownDescription: Enforces a path for new files to be created in, regardless of path the user is currently navigated to within the collection file list. Relative to the path of the collection defined in collection. Defaults to the path within the collection the user is currently navigated to.

collection.description: Sets which collection this action is creating a file in. This is used when matching the value for schema. Defaults to the containing collection these `add_options` are configured in.
collection.markdownDescription: Sets which collection this action is creating a file in. This is used when matching the value for schema. Defaults to the containing collection these `add_options` are configured in.

schema.description: The schema that new files are created from with this action. This schema is not restricted to the containing collection, and is instead relative to the collection specified with collection. Defaults to default if schemas are configured for the collection.
schema.markdownDescription: The schema that new files are created from with this action. This schema is not restricted to the containing collection, and is instead relative to the collection specified with collection. Defaults to default if schemas are configured for the collection.

default_content_file.description: The path to a file used to populate the initial contents of a new file if no schemas are configured. We recommend using schemas, and this is ignored if a schema is available.
default_content_file.markdownDescription: The path to a file used to populate the initial contents of a new file if no schemas are configured. We recommend using schemas, and this is ignored if a schema is available.

anyOf.markdownDescription: An option for the add menu.

anyOf.title: HREF Add Option
anyOf.description: An option for the add menu that opens a link.

name.description: The text displayed for the menu item.
name.markdownDescription: The text displayed for the menu item.

icon.description: The icon next to the text in the menu item.
icon.markdownDescription: The icon next to the text in the menu item.

href.description: The link that opens when the option is clicked. Can either be an external or internal link. If internal, the link is relative to the current site.
href.markdownDescription: The link that opens when the option is clicked. Can either be an external or internal link. If internal, the link is relative to the current site.

anyOf.markdownDescription: An option for the add menu that opens a link.

add_options.markdownDescription: This key defines the options available in the _+ Add_ button dropdown at the top right of your _Collection browser_.

disable_add.description: This key toggles whether team members can use the _+ Add_ button in the top right of the _Collection browser_ to add files to a Collection.
disable_add.markdownDescription: This key toggles whether team members can use the _+ Add_ button in the top right of the _Collection browser_ to add files to a Collection.

disable_add_folder.description: This key toggles whether team members can use the _+ Add_ button in the top right of the _Collection browser_ to add subfolders to a Collection.
disable_add_folder.markdownDescription: This key toggles whether team members can use the _+ Add_ button in the top right of the _Collection browser_ to add subfolders to a Collection.

disable_file_actions.description: This key toggles whether team members can use the _+ Add_ button in the top right of the _Collection browser_ to add files to a Collection.
disable_file_actions.markdownDescription: This key toggles whether team members can use the _+ Add_ button in the top right of the _Collection browser_ to add files to a Collection.

new_preview_url.description: This key defines a new URL for previewing your unbuilt pages in the Visual Editor.
new_preview_url.markdownDescription: This key defines a new URL for previewing your unbuilt pages in the Visual Editor.

schemas.description: This key defines which Schemas are available to populate files in this Collection.
schemas.markdownDescription: This key defines which Schemas are available to populate files in this Collection.

additionalProperties.title: Schema
additionalProperties.description: Definitions for your schemas, which are the structured data formats for your content files.

path.description: The path to the schema file. Relative to the root folder of the site.
path.markdownDescription: The path to the schema file. Relative to the root folder of the site.

name.description: Displayed in the add menu when creating new files. Defaults to a formatted version of the key.
name.markdownDescription: Displayed in the add menu when creating new files. Defaults to a formatted version of the key.

icon.description: Displayed in the add menu when creating new files; also used as the icon for collection files if no other preview is found.
icon.markdownDescription: Displayed in the add menu when creating new files; also used as the icon for collection files if no other preview is found.

new_preview_url.description: Preview your unbuilt pages (e.g. drafts) to another page's output URL. The Visual Editor will load that URL, where Data Bindings and Previews are available to render your new page without saving.
new_preview_url.markdownDescription: Preview your unbuilt pages (e.g. drafts) to another page's output URL. The Visual Editor will load that URL, where Data Bindings and Previews are available to render your new page without saving.

reorder_inputs.description: If true, inputs are sorted to match when editing. Extra inputs are ordered after expected inputs, unless `remove_extra_inputs` is true.
reorder_inputs.markdownDescription: If true, inputs are sorted to match when editing. Extra inputs are ordered after expected inputs, unless `remove_extra_inputs` is true.

hide_extra_inputs.description: Hides unexpected inputs when editing. Has no effect if `remove_extra_inputs` is true. Defaults to false.
hide_extra_inputs.markdownDescription: Hides unexpected inputs when editing. Has no effect if `remove_extra_inputs` is true. Defaults to false.

remove_empty_inputs.description: If checked, empty inputs are removed from the source file on save. Removed inputs will be available for editing again, provided they are in the matching schema/structure.
remove_empty_inputs.markdownDescription: If checked, empty inputs are removed from the source file on save. Removed inputs will be available for editing again, provided they are in the matching schema/structure.

remove_extra_inputs.description: If checked, extra inputs are removed when editing.
remove_extra_inputs.markdownDescription: If checked, extra inputs are removed when editing.

additionalProperties.markdownDescription: Definitions for your schemas, which are the structured data formats for your content files.

schema_key.description: This key defines the name for the structured data key that references the Schema a file uses.
schema_key.markdownDescription: This key defines the name for the structured data key that references the Schema a file uses.

data_config.description: Controls what data sets are available to populate select and multiselect inputs.
data_config.markdownDescription: Controls what data sets are available to populate select and multiselect inputs.

label.description: Used to identify a pull request template when multiple pull request templates are available.
label.markdownDescription: Used to identify a pull request template when multiple pull request templates are available.

title.description: The default value for the pull request title.
title.markdownDescription: The default value for the pull request title.

body.description: The default value for the pull request body.
body.markdownDescription: The default value for the pull request body.

template_path.description: Used to specify a file on the Site whose contents will be used as the default value for the pull request body. This has no effect if `body` is defined.
template_path.markdownDescription: Used to specify a file on the Site whose contents will be used as the default value for the pull request body. This has no effect if `body` is defined.

version.description: Controls which schema this file is validated against. Defaults to the latest schema.
version.markdownDescription: Controls which schema this file is validated against. Defaults to the latest schema.

output.description: Generates the integration file in another folder. Not applicable to Jekyll, Hugo, and Eleventy. Defaults to the root folder.
output.markdownDescription: Generates the integration file in another folder. Not applicable to Jekyll, Hugo, and Eleventy. Defaults to the root folder.

paths:BuildCoupled.description: Paths to where new asset files are uploaded to. They also set the default path when choosing existing images, and linking to existing files. Each path is relative to global `source`.

data.description: Parent folder of all site data files.
data.markdownDescription: Parent folder of all site data files.

layouts.description: Parent folder of all site layout files. Only applies to Jekyll, Hugo, and Eleventy sites.
layouts.markdownDescription: Parent folder of all site layout files. Only applies to Jekyll, Hugo, and Eleventy sites.

includes.description: Parent folder of all includes, partials, or shortcode files. Only applies to Jekyll, Hugo, and Eleventy sites.
includes.markdownDescription: Parent folder of all includes, partials, or shortcode files. Only applies to Jekyll, Hugo, and Eleventy sites.

paths:BuildCoupled.markdownDescription: Paths to where new asset files are uploaded to. They also set the default path when choosing existing images, and linking to existing files. Each path is relative to global `source`.

path.description: This key defines the folder path for the collection key in which it is nested. The value for this key is relative to your Site `source`. Each Collection must have a unique path.
path.markdownDescription: This key defines the folder path for the collection key in which it is nested. The value for this key is relative to your Site `source`. Each Collection must have a unique path.

glob.description: This key defines globs which filter the files visible in the _Collection browser_ for a given Collection. Values in this array are relative to the Collection `path`.
glob.markdownDescription: This key defines globs which filter the files visible in the _Collection browser_ for a given Collection. Values in this array are relative to the Collection `path`.

icon.title: Icon
icon.description: Material Symbol icon names available in CloudCannon for UI elements and content previews.
icon.markdownDescription: Material Symbol icon names available in CloudCannon for UI elements and content previews.

type.documentation.title: Documentation
type.documentation.description: Configuration for documentation links displayed in the CloudCannon interface.

text.description: The visible text used in the link.
text.markdownDescription: The visible text used in the link.

icon.description: The icon displayed next to the link.
icon.markdownDescription: The icon displayed next to the link.

type.documentation.markdownDescription: Configuration for documentation links displayed in the CloudCannon interface.

EditorKey.title: Editor Key
EditorKey.description: The available editors in CloudCannon: visual, content, and data editors.
EditorKey.markdownDescription: The available editors in CloudCannon: visual, content, and data editors.

type.create.title: Create
type.create.description: Controls where new files are saved.

path.description: The raw template to be processed when creating files. Relative to the containing collection's path.
path.markdownDescription: The raw template to be processed when creating files. Relative to the containing collection's path.

extra_data.description: Adds to the available data placeholders coming from the file. Entry values follow the same format as path, and are processed sequentially before path. These values are not saved back to your file.
extra_data.markdownDescription: Adds to the available data placeholders coming from the file. Entry values follow the same format as path, and are processed sequentially before path. These values are not saved back to your file.

publish_to.description: Defines a target collection when publishing. When a file is published, the target collection's create definition is used instead.
publish_to.markdownDescription: Defines a target collection when publishing. When a file is published, the target collection's create definition is used instead.

type._inputs.markdownDescription: Controls the behavior and appearance of your inputs in all data editing interfaces.

anyOf.markdownDescription: A union of all input types that are known to the CloudCannon configuration schema.

documentation.markdownDescription: Provides a custom link for documentation for editors shown above input.

type.markdownDescription: Sets an input type, which controls how this input appears and behaves.

options.description: Options that are specific to Text Inputs.

placeholder.description: Text shown when this input has no value.
placeholder.markdownDescription: Text shown when this input has no value.

options.markdownDescription: Options that are specific to Text Inputs.

type._inputs.*.comment.description: Changes the subtext below the _Label_. Has no default. Supports a limited set of Markdown: links, bold, italic, subscript, superscript, and inline code elements are allowed.
type._inputs.*.comment.markdownDescription: Changes the subtext below the _Label_. Has no default. Supports a limited set of Markdown: links, bold, italic, subscript, superscript, and inline code elements are allowed.

type._inputs.*.context.description: Adds an expandable section of rich text below the input.

content.description: The rich text content shown when opened. Supports a limited set of Markdown.
content.markdownDescription: The rich text content shown when opened. Supports a limited set of Markdown.

title.description: The text shown when not open. Defaults to "Context" if unset.
title.markdownDescription: The text shown when not open. Defaults to "Context" if unset.

type._inputs.*.hidden.description: Toggles the visibility of this input.
type._inputs.*.hidden.markdownDescription: Toggles the visibility of this input.

type._inputs.*.disabled.markdownDescription: Toggles if this input can be edited.

type._inputs.*.instance_value.title: Instance Value
type._inputs.*.instance_value.description: Controls if and how the value of this input is instantiated when created. This occurs when creating files, or adding array items containing the configured input.
type._inputs.*.instance_value.markdownDescription: Controls if and how the value of this input is instantiated when created. This occurs when creating files, or adding array items containing the configured input.

type._inputs.*.disable_instance_value_re.description: Prevents the default where inputs configured with an `instance_value` are rehydrated with a new value when duplicated in the CMS.
type._inputs.*.disable_instance_value_re.markdownDescription: Prevents the default where inputs configured with an `instance_value` are rehydrated with a new value when duplicated in the CMS.

type._inputs.*.cascade.description: Specifies whether or not this input configuration should be merged with any matching, less specific configuration.
type._inputs.*.cascade.markdownDescription: Specifies whether or not this input configuration should be merged with any matching, less specific configuration.

type._inputs.*.options.max_length.description: This key defines the maximum string length, in characters, that CloudCannon will allow in an Input. When configured, CloudCannon will warn you when an Input value is too long. If the Input already contains a longer value, CloudCannon will require you to remove characters until the Input contains a valid string to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes.

If the Input already contains a longer value, CloudCannon will require you to remove characters until the Input contains a valid string to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes.

type._inputs.*.options.max_length_messag.description: This key defines the message that explains which maximum string length an Input will accept. This key requires you to define `options.max_length`.
type._inputs.*.options.max_length_messag.markdownDescription: This key defines the message that explains which maximum string length an Input will accept. This key requires you to define `options.max_length`.

type._inputs.*.options.min_length.description: This key defines the minimum string length, in characters, that CloudCannon will allow in an Input. When configured, CloudCannon will warn you when an Input value is too short. If the Input already contains a shorter value, CloudCannon will require you to add characters until the Input contains a valid string to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes.

type._inputs.*.options.pattern.description: This key defines a regular expression that the Input value must match. When configured, CloudCannon will require you to enter a value that matches the REGEX pattern. If the Input already contains an invalid value, CloudCannon will require you to enter a valid string to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes.
type._inputs.*.options.pattern.markdownDescription: This key defines a regular expression that the Input value must match. If the Input already contains an invalid value, CloudCannon will require you to enter a valid string to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes.

type._inputs.*.options.pattern_message.description: This key defines the message that explains which regular expression an Input will accept. This key requires you to define `options.pattern`.
type._inputs.*.options.pattern_message.markdownDescription: This key defines the message that explains which regular expression an Input will accept. This key requires you to define `options.pattern`.

type._inputs.*.options.pattern_flags.description: This key defines the flags (e.g. case-insensitive searching) for the regular expression set in `options.pattern`.

multiline.description: `m` - `^` and `$` match the start and end of each line rather than the entire string.
multiline.markdownDescription: `m` - `^` and `$` match the start and end of each line rather than the entire string.

unicode.description: `u` - Pattern is treated as a sequence of Unicode code points.
unicode.markdownDescription: `u` - Pattern is treated as a sequence of Unicode code points.

type._inputs.*.options.pattern_flags.markdownDescription: This key defines the flags (e.g. case-insensitive searching) for the regular expression set in `options.pattern`.

type._inputs.*.options.required.description: This key toggles whether CloudCannon requires this Input to have a value. If set to true, CloudCannon will require you to enter a value to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes.
type._inputs.*.options.required.markdownDescription: This key toggles whether CloudCannon requires this Input to have a value. If set to true, CloudCannon will require you to enter a value to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes.

type._inputs.*.options.mime_type.title: Mime Type

type._inputs.*.options.mime_type.markdownDescription: Sets the format images are converted to prior to upload. The extension of the file is updated to match. Defaults to keeping the mime type of the uploaded file.

type._inputs.*.options.resize_style.title: Resize Style
type._inputs.*.options.resize_style.description: Sets how uploaded image files are resized with a bounding box defined by width and height prior to upload. Has no effect when selecting existing images, or if width and height are unset.
type._inputs.*.options.resize_style.markdownDescription: Sets how uploaded image files are resized with a bounding box defined by width and height prior to upload. Has no effect when selecting existing images, or if width and height are unset.

type._inputs.*.options.width.title: Width
type._inputs.*.options.width.description: Defines the width of the bounding box used in the image resizing process defined with resize_style.
type._inputs.*.options.width.markdownDescription: Defines the width of the bounding box used in the image resizing process defined with resize_style.

type._inputs.*.options.height.markdownDescription: Defines the height of the bounding box used in the image resizing process defined with resize_style.

type._inputs.*.options.expandable.title: Expandable
type._inputs.*.options.expandable.description: Controls whether or not images can be upscaled to fit the bounding box during resize prior to upload. Has no effect if files are not resized.
type._inputs.*.options.expandable.markdownDescription: Controls whether or not images can be upscaled to fit the bounding box during resize prior to upload. Has no effect if files are not resized.

type._inputs.*.options.image_size_attrib.title: Image Size Attributes

type._inputs.*.options.image_size_attrib.markdownDescription: Instructs the editor to save `width` and `height` attributes on the image elements. This can prevent pop-in as a page loads.

type._editables.*.underline.markdownDescription: Enables a control to underline selected text.

DateInput.title: Date Input
DateInput.description: Provides an editing interface for date and/or time values.

documentation.description: Provides a custom link for documentation for editors shown above input.
documentation.markdownDescription: Provides a custom link for documentation for editors shown above input.

type.title: Type
type.description: Sets an input type, which controls how this input appears and behaves.
type.markdownDescription: Sets an input type, which controls how this input appears and behaves.

options.description: Options that are specific to Date Inputs.

timezone.description: Specifies the time zone that dates are displayed and edited in. Also changes the suffix the date is persisted to the file with. Defaults to the global `timezone`.
timezone.markdownDescription: Specifies the time zone that dates are displayed and edited in. Also changes the suffix the date is persisted to the file with. Defaults to the global `timezone`.

start_from.description: This key defines the earliest date and time, inclusive, that CloudCannon will allow in an Input. When configured, CloudCannon will prevent you from selecting an earlier date and time. If the Input already contains an earlier date and time, CloudCannon will require you to change it to a valid value to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes. Value must be in ISO8601 format. If `options.end_before` is also configured, this key cannot be a later date and time.
start_from.markdownDescription: This key defines the earliest date and time, inclusive, that CloudCannon will allow in an Input. When configured, CloudCannon will prevent you from selecting an earlier date and time. If the Input already contains an earlier date and time, CloudCannon will require you to change it to a valid value to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes. Value must be in ISO8601 format. If `options.end_before` is also configured, this key cannot be a later date and time.

start_from_message.description: This key defines the message that explains why an Input needs to have a start date. This key requires you to define `options.start_from`.
start_from_message.markdownDescription: This key defines the message that explains why an Input needs to have a start date. This key requires you to define `options.start_from`.

end_before.description: This key defines the date and time, exclusive, that CloudCannon will allow in an Input. When configured, CloudCannon will prevent you from selecting a later date and time. If the Input already contains a later date and time, CloudCannon will require you to change it to a valid value to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes. Value must be in ISO8601 format. If options.start_from is also configured, this key cannot be an earlier date and time. This key has no default.
end_before.markdownDescription: This key defines the date and time, exclusive, that CloudCannon will allow in an Input. When configured, CloudCannon will prevent you from selecting a later date and time. If the Input already contains a later date and time, CloudCannon will require you to change it to a valid value to save your changes, or discard your unsaved changes. Value must be in ISO8601 format. If options.start_from is also configured, this key cannot be an earlier date and time. This key has no default.

end_before_message.description: This key defines the message that explains why an Input needs to have an end date. This key requires you to define `options.end_before`. This key has no default.
end_before_message.markdownDescription: This key defines the message that explains why an Input needs to have an end date. This key requires you to define `options.end_before`. This key has no default.

markdown.title: Markdown Settings
markdown.description: Configuration for Markdown processing engines and formatting options.

engine.description: The flavor of Markdown to use to convert between HTML and Markdown.
engine.markdownDescription: The flavor of Markdown to use to convert between HTML and Markdown.

typographer.description: Enable some language-neutral replacement, as well as quotes beautification.
typographer.markdownDescription: Enable some language-neutral replacement, as well as quotes beautification.

quotes.description: Double + single quotes replacement pairs, when typographer enabled and smartquotes on. For example, you can use "«»„"" for Russian, ""„"‚"" for German, and ["«\xA0", "\xA0»", "‹\xA0", "\xA0›"] for French (including `nbsp`).
quotes.markdownDescription: Double + single quotes replacement pairs, when typographer enabled and smartquotes on. For example, you can use "«»„"" for Russian, ""„"‚"" for German, and ["«\xA0", "\xA0»", "‹\xA0", "\xA0›"] for French (including `nbsp`).

spaced_lists.description: Output lists with an extra space in Markdown.
spaced_lists.markdownDescription: Output lists with an extra space in Markdown.

sentence_per_line.description: Add linebreaks between sentences in Markdown.
sentence_per_line.markdownDescription: Add linebreaks between sentences in Markdown.

code_block_fences.description: Determines which style of code block fences to use.
code_block_fences.markdownDescription: Determines which style of code block fences to use.

treat_indentation_as_code.description: Determines whether 4 spaces on indentation should be read as a code block.
treat_indentation_as_code.markdownDescription: Determines whether 4 spaces on indentation should be read as a code block.

escape_snippets_in_code_blocks.description: Render snippets as plain text within code blocks.
escape_snippets_in_code_blocks.markdownDescription: Render snippets as plain text within code blocks.

strikethrough.description: Output strikes in wrapped in double tildes (e.g. `~~strike~~`).
strikethrough.markdownDescription: Output strikes in wrapped in double tildes (e.g. `~~strike~~`).

subscript.description: Output subscript in wrapped in tildes (e.g. `~sub~`).
subscript.markdownDescription: Output subscript in wrapped in tildes (e.g. `~sub~`).

superscript.description: Output superscript in wrapped in carets (e.g. `^super^`).
superscript.markdownDescription: Output superscript in wrapped in carets (e.g. `^super^`).

attributes.description: Save element attributes in Markdown format instead of converting to HTML.

template.markdownDescription: The template that this snippet should inherit, out of the available Snippet Templates.

inline.description: Whether this snippet can appear inline (within a sentence). Defaults to false, which will treat this snippet as a block-level element in the content editor.
inline.markdownDescription: Whether this snippet can appear inline (within a sentence). Defaults to false, which will treat this snippet as a block-level element in the content editor.

view.markdownDescription: Controls how selected items are rendered. Defaults to 'card', or 'inline' if `inline` is true.

strict_whitespace.description: Whether this snippet treats whitespace as-is or not.
